title: Everyday verbs: choose the right word
lesson: Choose the verb that matches the action in each sentence. These are common everyday actions.

items.text: I {1} an apple for breakfast.
items.explanation: You eat solid food like an apple.

items.text: She likes to {1} cold water after running.
items.explanation: You drink liquids like water.

items.text: The baby will {1} for eight hours tonight.
items.explanation: You sleep when you rest at night.

items.text: Every morning I {1} fast in the park.
items.explanation: You run when you move quickly on your feet.

items.text: He likes to {1} a book before bed.
items.explanation: You read a book to look at the words.

items.text: Please {1} your name on this paper.
items.explanation: You write your name with a pen.

items.text: Can you {1} the door, please? It is too hot.
items.explanation: You open a door to let air in.

items.text: It is cold, so please {1} the window.
items.explanation: You close a window to keep the cold out.

items.text: We {1} fresh bread at the shop every day.
items.explanation: You buy things at a shop with money.

items.text: My father can {1} a car very well.
items.explanation: You drive a car on the road.

items.text: My mother will {1} dinner in the kitchen.
items.explanation: You cook food in the kitchen.

items.text: We {1} to school every morning because it is near.
items.explanation: You walk when you go somewhere on foot.

items.text: I like to {1} to music on my phone.
items.explanation: You listen to music with your ears.

items.text: On Sunday we {1} films on television.
items.explanation: You watch films with your eyes.

items.text: She can {1} three languages.
items.explanation: You speak a language with your voice.

items.text: Please {1} your hands before dinner.
items.explanation: You wash your hands with water and soap.

items.text: The children {1} football in the garden.
items.explanation: You play a game like football.

items.text: I can {1} in the sea on a hot day.
items.explanation: You swim in water like the sea.

items.text: Please {1} down on this chair.
items.explanation: You sit down on a chair.

items.text: Birds {1} high in the sky.
items.explanation: Birds fly in the air with their wings.

items.text: Can you {1} me the salt, please?
items.explanation: You give something by passing it to a person.

items.text: I {1} my grandmother on the phone every week.
items.explanation: You call someone to talk on the phone.

items.text: She likes to {1} pictures of animals.
items.explanation: You draw pictures with a pen or pencil.

items.text: We {1} our friends in the city centre.
items.explanation: You meet friends when you see them somewhere.

items.text: He likes to {1} happy songs in the shower.
items.explanation: You sing songs with your voice.

items.text: I {1} you because you are my best friend.
items.explanation: You love people who are very important to you.

items.text: Please {1} the light when you leave the room.
items.explanation: You turn off the light to stop it shining.

items.text: The dog likes to {1} for the ball.
items.explanation: You jump when you push up off the ground.

items.text: I {1} my keys on the table by the door.
items.explanation: You put something when you place it somewhere.

items.text: Can you {1} this heavy box for me?
items.explanation: You carry a heavy box by holding and moving it.

items.text: I {1} a new word in English today.
items.explanation: You learn when you get new knowledge.

items.text: She likes to {1} other children with their homework.
items.explanation: You help others by doing something good for them.

items.text: Please {1} for me at the bus stop.
items.explanation: You wait until someone or something arrives.

items.text: We {1} the table after we finish dinner.
items.explanation: You clean the table to make it tidy.
